folio.label: 187r
folio.l: And putten hi~ to prisou~ , in swicú wyse
As ©e han herd , & mete & drynk he hadde
So smal , that wel vnethe , it may suffise
And ther witú al , it was ful poore & badde
¢ And oon a day bifel , that in that houre
Whan æ his mete , wont was to be brougút
The Gayler shette the dores , of the Tour
He herd it wel , but he spak rigút nougút
And in his herte , a noon æäe fel a thougút
That they for honger , wolde don hi~ dyen
Allas q¶ he , allas æ I was wrougút
There witú the teres , fellen from hise eyen
¢ His ©onge Sone , æ thre ©er was of age
Vn to hi~ seide , fader, fader , why do ©e wepe
Whan wyl the gailler , bryngen oure potage
Is æere no mussel bred , that ©e do kepe
I am so hungry , that I may nat slepe
Now wolde god , æ I mygút slepen euäe
Tha~ne shulde nougút hunger , in my wombe crepe
There is no thyngö but bred , æ me were leuäe
¢ Thus day by day , this child bygan to crie
Til in his fadres barm , a doun it lay
And seide , fare wel fader , I mot dye
And kist his fader , & dyed the same day
And whan the wooful fader , ded it say
For woo , hise armes two , he gan to byte
And seide allas fortune , and weylaway
Thy false whel , my woo al may I wyte
¢ Hise children wend , æ it for hunger was
That he hise armes gnow , & nat for woo
And seiden fader , do nat so allas
But rathere ete the flessú , vp oon vs two
Oure flessú æè ©af vs , take oure flessú vs froo
And ete I now , rigút thus , æei to hi~ seide
And after æ , w Inne a day or two
They leide he~ in his lappe a doun , & deide
¢ Hi~ self dispeired eke , for hunger starf
Thus ended is , this mygúty Erl of Pise
From heigú estat , fortune a wey hi~ karf
Of this tragedye , it ougút I now suffise
Who so wol heere it , in a lengere wyse
Redetú the grete Poete , of Ytaille
That higút Daunt , for he can it al deuyse
Fro poynt to poynt , nat oo word wil he faille
Here endetú the Monkes Tale , de casib virorum Illustriu~

note.notetxt: double check. The abbreviation of casib/ODR/
L FRThe rubric is written in the footer of the folio.ODR
